title: Fix: PRAGMA foreign_keys only applies to first pool connection
description:
High-severity bug: sql.Open returns a connection pool. PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON is connection-scoped — it only applies to the first connection. New pool connections don't have FK enforcement, so DELETE FROM sessions won't cascade to session_tags, leaving orphaned rows.

Solution: set db.SetMaxOpenConns(1) to force a single connection (appropriate for a local CLI tool), or use a connection init hook via the driver's DSN parameters.
requirements: In internal/db/db.go Open(): after sql.Open, call sqlDB.SetMaxOpenConns(1) to ensure all PRAGMAs apply consistently across the single connection. This is appropriate for a CLI tool that doesn't need concurrent DB access.
Add a comment explaining why MaxOpenConns is set to 1 (PRAGMA enforcement)
Verify the project compiles with 'go build ./cmd/ccs/' and all tests pass with 'go test ./...'
